created_from: User prompt: what is the minimal conformal non-abelian model that can explain the NANOGrav signal?
warnings: Candidate only: no FOPT calculation, PTA scan, or NANOGrav fit has been performed at proposal stage.
No existing output handoff files were found, so duplicate checking was limited to an empty local output set.
The scalar thermal mass coefficient is left symbolic as c_phi pending downstream backend convention choices.
approximations: Classically scale-invariant Coleman-Weinberg treatment with one radial background field chi.
Portal couplings and extra matter are neglected to keep the non-Abelian conformal model minimal.
The CW pseudo-dilaton contribution is treated as dependent and not double-counted as an independent scanned bosonic mass ratio.

gauge_groups.name: SU(2)_D
gauge_groups.generators: T_D_a = sigma_a / 2 in the fundamental representation

gauge_groups.name: SM
gauge_groups.notes: All new dark-sector fields are Standard Model singlets.

global_symmetries.name: classical_scale_invariance
global_symmetries.notes: Forbids explicit scalar mass terms; scale generated radiatively through Coleman-Weinberg symmetry breaking.

particle_content.name: Phi
particle_content.components: One complex SU(2)_D doublet; in unitary gauge Phi = (0, chi / sqrt(2))^T.
particle_content.notes: Provides the single conformal radial background chi for the FOPT backend.

particle_content.name: W_D_a
particle_content.notes: Three massive dark vector bosons after SU(2)_D breaking.

particle_content.name: chi
particle_content.notes: Coleman-Weinberg pseudo-dilaton; mass is dependent, not an independent scanned input.

lagrangian_terms.name: dark_gauge_kinetic
lagrangian_terms.gauge_invariance_notes: Adjoint field strength contracted over SU(2)_D adjoint and Lorentz indices.

lagrangian_terms.name: scalar_kinetic
lagrangian_terms.gauge_invariance_notes: Fundamental doublet covariant derivative contracted with its Hermitian conjugate.

lagrangian_terms.name: conformal_tree_potential
lagrangian_terms.gauge_invariance_notes: Only renormalizable SU(2)_D-invariant scalar potential term for one doublet under classical scale invariance.

free_parameters.name: gD
free_parameters.range: positive perturbative gauge coupling; downstream scans should impose perturbativity and beta_lambda > 0

free_parameters.name: vD
free_parameters.range: positive energy scale to be scanned by downstream FOPT/PTA stages
free_parameters.role: Coleman-Weinberg vev of the radial field chi.

vD.description: Zero-temperature Coleman-Weinberg vev in the chi direction.

beta_lambda.description: Model-independent CW beta_lambda from the three massive dark gauge bosons with gb = gD / 2 and total vector dof 9.

lambda_phi.determination: Coleman-Weinberg minimization condition at the chosen renormalization scale; not an independent scan parameter for semianalytic_pipeline.
lambda_phi.description: Running quartic in V0 = lambda_phi * chi**4 / 4.

m_WD.description: Common physical mass of the three dark gauge bosons after symmetry breaking.

gb_WD.description: Mass-over-background ratio for each dark vector, m_WD(chi) = gb_WD * chi.

gb_scalar.description: Dependent pseudo-dilaton mass-over-vev ratio; not independently scanned or double-counted in beta_lambda.

portal_coupling.description: Higgs portal neglected in the minimal proposal.

assumptions: The model is considered as a dark sector with negligible portal coupling to the Standard Model for the proposal stage.
The phase transition is assumed to be controlled by the single radial background chi.
Gauge fixing and daisy-resummation details are deferred to downstream FOPT implementation conventions.
No quantitative claim about explaining NANOGrav is made before downstream scans.

backend_compatibility.notes: Classically scale-invariant single-field model with no explicit tree-level mass term. All relevant masses are proportional to chi. Warning status records that the PTA signal has not yet been computed.

ssb_pattern.notes: A single complex fundamental doublet completely Higgses SU(2)_D.

vev_convention.notes: With this convention, m_WD2(chi) = gD**2 * chi**2 / 4 and V0(chi) = lambda_phi * chi**4 / 4.

tree_level_potential.name: conformal_quartic
tree_level_potential.notes: No mass term due to classical scale invariance; lambda_phi is CW-fixed and dependent.

field_dependent_masses.notes: Three SU(2)_D gauge bosons, each with three massive polarizations after symmetry breaking.
Dependent CW pseudo-dilaton entry for bookkeeping only; do not use as an independent beta_lambda source.

thermal_masses.notes: Symbolic scalar thermal mass coefficient to be fixed by downstream FOPT backend conventions.
Symbolic Debye mass coefficient for longitudinal dark gauge modes; downstream implementation may choose the appropriate SU(2) coefficient.

expression_symbols.meaning: Gauge-invariant scalar bilinear Phi dagger Phi.
Hermitian conjugate gauge-covariant derivative acting on Phi.
